refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable operators and the system to achieve and maintain energy efficiency performance such that measured energy_per_ton (energy_used / ton_moved) remains at or below the customer-defined E_target throughout the evaluation period, under nominal operating conditions and across the range of supported worksite scenarios.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-003 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that must be refined from a vague 'support achieving' statement into a measurable, verifiable functional requirement. The refinement explicitly includes the measurement formula, the target boundary condition (≤ E_target), the evaluation period context, and operational scope. This clarifies that energy efficiency is a system-level emergent property dependent on vehicle efficiency, operational strategy, and teleoperation overhead.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Establish a baseline energy consumption profile (kWh or fuel equivalent) and ton-moved tally across a representative evaluation period. Calculate energy_per_ton at regular intervals (daily, weekly, cumulative). Compare against E_target. Pass if cumulative energy_per_ton ≤ E_target; fail if it exceeds target. Verification must account for worksite variability, operator skill, and network conditions.
sub_frs: FR-EE-001: The vehicle shall minimize energy consumption per unit distance traveled under load.
FR-EE-002: The system shall monitor and report real-time energy consumption and work output (tons moved).
FR-EE-003: The teleoperation subsystem shall minimize energy overhead (network, compute, latency) to avoid degrading overall efficiency.
FR-EE-004: The system shall provide operator guidance and feedback to encourage energy-efficient operational practices.
FR-EE-005: The system shall log and aggregate energy and productivity metrics for post-evaluation analysis.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement a closed-loop energy management system that monitors real-time power draw, work output, and efficiency metrics; provide operator feedback and automated efficiency optimization (e.g., load balancing, route optimization, idle reduction).

DP-B: Equip the vehicle with high-efficiency drivetrain components (motor, transmission, hydraulics) and implement predictive maintenance to minimize energy waste; couple with telemetry-based efficiency tracking.

DP-C: Integrate energy efficiency as a multi-objective optimization problem: minimize energy_per_ton via vehicle design, operational strategy, and teleoperation overhead reduction; use real-time telemetry and historical data to guide operator decisions.

DP-D: Establish energy efficiency as a system-level KPI managed through a combination of vehicle efficiency improvements, operational best practices (training, procedures), and continuous monitoring with corrective feedback loops.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Integrate energy efficiency as a multi-objective optimization problem: minimize energy_per_ton via vehicle design, operational strategy, and teleoperation overhead reduction; use real-time telemetry and historical data to guide operator decisions.

potential_sources: Vehicle drivetrain efficiency (DP-B concern) is decoupled from operational strategy and teleoperation overhead (DP-C concern) via the telemetry interface; each can be optimized independently.
Teleoperation overhead is explicitly included in DP-C, avoiding hidden coupling between remote operation cost and overall efficiency target.
Real-time feedback and historical data enable independent optimization of operator behavior without requiring redesign of vehicle hardware.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C is selected because it explicitly treats energy efficiency as a system-level property emerging from vehicle design, operational decisions, and teleoperation efficiency. It avoids the trap of optimizing only vehicle hardware (DP-B) or only feedback (DP-A) in isolation. The multi-objective framing ensures that teleoperation overhead is not overlooked.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Real-time telemetry (power, work output, position, load) is available with sufficient fidelity and latency to enable meaningful operator feedback and automated optimization.
Assumption: Operator behavior can be influenced by feedback and guidance without requiring extensive retraining or procedural changes.
Risk: If telemetry is delayed or inaccurate, feedback-driven optimization may be ineffective or counterproductive.
Risk: Teleoperation overhead (network latency, compute, bandwidth) may be significant enough to materially impact energy_per_ton; if not actively managed, it could prevent target achievement.
Risk: Vehicle hardware efficiency may be insufficient to meet E_target even with optimal operations; this would require hardware redesign outside the scope of this DP.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-C is sound but should be refined to explicitly state the optimization hierarchy: (1) vehicle drivetrain efficiency (hardware baseline), (2) operational strategy optimization (routing, load balancing, idle reduction), (3) teleoperation overhead minimization (network efficiency, compute efficiency), (4) operator feedback and training. This clarifies dependencies and enables phased implementation.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary encompassing all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and their integration. It is responsible for orchestrating energy efficiency optimization across all subsystems, aggregating telemetry, computing energy_per_ton KPI, and providing system-level reporting and feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Inbound telemetry from Vehicle (power consumption, work output, position, load, operational state) and Teleoperation (network latency, compute overhead, bandwidth usage). Used to compute energy_per_ton and drive optimization decisions.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Outbound tasking commands from NGHE Solution to Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems, including efficiency optimization directives (e.g., route suggestions, load balancing, idle reduction), operator guidance, and operational parameters.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional safety alerts between NGHE Solution and Safety Services. Inbound alerts from Safety Services regarding safety constraints that may impact energy efficiency (e.g., speed limits, restricted zones). Outbound alerts from NGHE Solution if efficiency optimization conflicts with safety requirements.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional network connectivity for teleoperation and remote monitoring. NGHE Solution monitors network efficiency metrics (latency, bandwidth, packet loss) to quantify teleoperation overhead and optimize network usage.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for energy efficiency KPI management. It integrates telemetry from all subsystems, computes energy_per_ton, and orchestrates optimization. Minimal diffusion: only the actual energy measurement hardware (power sensors in Vehicle) and network monitoring (in Teleoperation) are external; the KPI logic and optimization orchestration are fully contained.

Vehicle contains drivetrain efficiency (hardware) and can implement local operational optimizations (idle reduction, load balancing). However, energy_per_ton is a system-level KPI that requires integration with work output (tons moved) and teleoperation overhead; these are not fully contained in Vehicle. Assigning DP-C to Vehicle alone would diffuse the optimization logic across Vehicle and other subsystems.

Teleoperation can optimize its own overhead (network efficiency, compute efficiency) but cannot independently achieve energy_per_ton target without visibility into vehicle efficiency and work output. Assigning DP-C to Teleoperation alone would create high diffusion and coupling.

Safety Services is orthogonal to energy efficiency optimization. While safety constraints may limit efficiency optimization (e.g., speed limits), Safety Services should not own the energy efficiency KPI logic. High diffusion if assigned here.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate owner of DP-C (multi-objective energy efficiency optimization) because it is the only module with visibility into all three optimization dimensions: vehicle efficiency (via telemetry), work output (via telemetry), and teleoperation overhead (via network monitoring). The NGHE Solution can aggregate these signals, compute energy_per_ton, and issue optimization directives to Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems without diffusing the core optimization logic.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution becomes a 'god module' that orchestrates too many subsystems, it may become a bottleneck or single point of failure. Mitigation: Define clear, narrow interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) and delegate implementation details to subsystems.

Risk: Real-time energy_per_ton computation and optimization may require low-latency processing; if NGHE Solution is implemented as a remote cloud service, latency may degrade feedback effectiveness. Mitigation: Implement a hybrid architecture with local edge compute for real-time feedback and cloud compute for historical analysis.

Risk: Telemetry fidelity and availability are critical; if Vehicle or Teleoperation subsystems do not provide accurate, timely telemetry, NGHE Solution cannot compute reliable energy_per_ton. Mitigation: Define telemetry requirements and SLAs in subsystem contracts.

Risk: Safety constraints (from Safety Services) may conflict with energy efficiency optimization; NGHE Solution must prioritize safety over efficiency. Mitigation: Implement safety override logic and ensure SafetyAlert interface is bidirectional and monitored.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign DP-C to NGHE Solution with the following refinements: (1) Define a telemetry schema that includes power consumption, work output, position, load, network metrics, and operational state. (2) Specify the energy_per_ton computation algorithm and update frequency. (3) Define the optimization directives that NGHE Solution can issue to Vehicle and Teleoperation (e.g., route suggestions, idle reduction, network optimization). (4) Establish SLAs for telemetry latency and accuracy. (5) Implement a safety override mechanism to ensure SafetyAlert takes precedence over efficiency optimization.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR-EE (energy_per_ton ≤ E_target) is directly addressed by DP-C (multi-objective optimization). DP-C explicitly includes vehicle efficiency, operational strategy, and teleoperation overhead—the three levers needed to achieve the FR. The mapping is clear and non-redundant: each element of DP-C contributes to reducing energy_per_ton. No independence violations detected.

diffusion_risks: Moderate risk: DP-C requires coordination across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. If interfaces are poorly defined, optimization logic may diffuse across multiple modules. Mitigation: Enforce strict interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) and centralize optimization logic in NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate container for DP-C. It has visibility into all three optimization dimensions and can orchestrate subsystem behavior without diffusing core logic. Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems implement local optimizations (drivetrain efficiency, network efficiency) and report telemetry; NGHE Solution aggregates and optimizes at the system level.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-EE is feasible if telemetry is available. Energy consumption can be measured via power sensors in Vehicle; work output (tons moved) can be tracked via load sensors and position tracking. Energy_per_ton can be computed and compared against E_target. Verification requires an agreed evaluation period and baseline conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns the energy efficiency KPI and optimization logic. Vehicle owns drivetrain efficiency and local operational optimizations. Teleoperation owns network efficiency. Safety Services owns safety constraints. Each module has a clear role and interface.

recommended_revisions: Refine the telemetry schema to ensure all required signals (power, work output, position, load, network metrics) are defined and available.
Establish SLAs for telemetry latency and accuracy to ensure real-time feedback is effective.
Define the optimization algorithm and update frequency for energy_per_ton computation.
Implement a safety override mechanism to ensure SafetyAlert takes precedence over efficiency optimization.
Plan for hybrid edge/cloud compute architecture to balance real-time feedback with historical analysis.

decision.justification: Refined FR-EE is sound and DP-C is well-selected with clear mapping to NGHE Solution. However, DP-C is not atomic; it encompasses three distinct optimization dimensions (vehicle efficiency, operational strategy, teleoperation overhead) that require further decomposition. Zigzagging down will enable detailed design of each optimization lever and ensure that sub-FRs are properly allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. This is necessary to avoid diffusion and ensure clear ownership boundaries.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-EE-001: The vehicle shall minimize energy consumption per unit distance traveled under load (allocated to Vehicle module).

FR-EE-002: The system shall monitor and report real-time energy consumption and work output (tons moved) with latency ≤ [TBD] and accuracy ≥ [TBD]% (allocated to NGHE Solution with support from Vehicle and Teleoperation).

FR-EE-003: The teleoperation subsystem shall minimize energy overhead (network, compute, latency) such that teleoperation overhead ≤ [TBD]% of total energy consumption (allocated to Teleoperation module).

FR-EE-004: The system shall provide operator guidance and feedback to encourage energy-efficient operational practices, with feedback latency ≤ [TBD] (allocated to NGHE Solution with support from Vehicle and Teleoperation).
FR-EE-005: The system shall log and aggregate energy and productivity metrics for post-evaluation analysis and continuous improvement (allocated to NGHE Solution).

assumptions: Real-time telemetry (power, work output, position, load, network metrics) is available with sufficient fidelity and latency to enable meaningful operator feedback and automated optimization.
Operator behavior can be influenced by feedback and guidance without requiring extensive retraining or procedural changes.
Vehicle hardware efficiency is sufficient to meet E_target when combined with optimal operations; if not, hardware redesign will be required.
Teleoperation overhead is quantifiable and can be minimized through network and compute optimization.
Safety constraints (from Safety Services) can be integrated with energy efficiency optimization without fundamental conflicts.
open_risks: Telemetry latency or inaccuracy may degrade feedback effectiveness and prevent target achievement.
Teleoperation overhead may be significant enough to materially impact energy_per_ton; if not actively managed, it could prevent target achievement.
Vehicle hardware efficiency may be insufficient to meet E_target even with optimal operations; this would require hardware redesign outside the scope of current DP.
Safety constraints may conflict with energy efficiency optimization; prioritization and trade-off resolution needed.
Operator adoption of efficiency guidance may be lower than expected; behavioral factors and training effectiveness are uncertain.
evidence_needed: Baseline energy consumption profile for representative worksite scenarios and operator skill levels.
Telemetry system specification (sensors, latency, accuracy) and validation data.
Teleoperation overhead quantification (network latency, bandwidth, compute cost) under various network conditions.
Vehicle drivetrain efficiency data and potential for optimization.
Operator feedback effectiveness study (e.g., A/B testing of guidance strategies).
Safety constraint analysis to identify potential conflicts with energy efficiency optimization.
